thesis: Existing Fab 12i and P3 ramp are the primary near-term Southeast Asia priority.
best_fit_role: Front-end manufacturing anchor and customer qualification base.
unique_advantage: Direct operational control, customer assurance, mature supplier base, and Fab 12i execution leverage.
unique_risk: High cost, utilities pressure, and management distraction if additional external options are opened too early.
move_up_triggers: P3 ramp remains the central constraint for customers, utilization, and margin improvement.
move_down_triggers: P3 reaches stable ramp and external options show validated customer demand, partner economics, incentives, and ROIC.
evidence_gaps: P3 ramp milestones by customer and product family
Utilization and margin thresholds for reopening external Stage 1 options

thesis: Strong non-Singapore packaging / OSAT ecosystem watchlist.
best_fit_role: Packaging / OSAT coordination, not greenfield owned investment.
unique_advantage: Penang / Kulim semiconductor ecosystem, OSAT density, and policy support.
unique_risk: Customer overlap, attach-rate, partner economics, and incentives remain unvalidated.
move_up_triggers: UMC customer overlap, packaging attach-rate, partner economics, incentives, and ROIC are validated.
move_down_triggers: Fab 12i P3 ramp absorbs capital or no customer-specific packaging demand emerges.
evidence_gaps: Named UMC wafer flows needing Malaysia packaging or test

best_fit_role: Customer engineering or account operations linked to EMS and electronics clusters.
unique_advantage: Large electronics manufacturing footprint and improving semiconductor policy attention.
unique_risk: Ecosystem relevance to UMC customers is still proxy-based.
move_up_triggers: Named Fab 12i customers require local technical support near Vietnam EMS flows.
move_down_triggers: Demand is limited to account coverage or generic support that can be handled from Singapore.
evidence_gaps: UMC customer overlap in Vietnam EMS clusters
Local FAE and semiconductor quality support depth

thesis: Automotive, power, and industrial specialty-process watchlist.
best_fit_role: Demand sensing and selective account coverage for specialty mature-node customers.
unique_advantage: Automotive, industrial, electronics, and logistics base in Bangkok and the Eastern Economic Corridor.
unique_risk: Policy and demand signals do not yet prove UMC-specific revenue.
move_up_triggers: Named automotive, power, or industrial customers validate specialty-process demand.
move_down_triggers: Customer overlap remains thin or policy execution does not translate into semiconductor demand.
evidence_gaps: Specialty process demand by product family

thesis: Customer support and account operations watchlist.
best_fit_role: Support hub, account operations, quality triage, or documentation support.
unique_advantage: English-language technical workforce and established electronics / back-end semiconductor operations.
unique_risk: Support role may be too generic unless tied to Fab 12i customer workflows.
move_up_triggers: Specific Fab 12i customer workflows require Philippines-based support capacity.
move_down_triggers: The opportunity is generic back-office support or can be handled from Singapore or Malaysia.
evidence_gaps: Escalation model tied to Fab 12i products

best_fit_role: Policy monitoring, market development, and long-term design ecosystem tracking.
unique_advantage: Large domestic market and long-term automotive, consumer, EV, and industrial demand potential.
unique_risk: Policy signals do not yet imply manufacturing readiness or UMC-specific customer density.
move_up_triggers: IC design, policy, or end-market programs create named UMC-relevant mature-node demand.
move_down_triggers: Policy programs remain broad and do not create customer demand or partner maturity.
evidence_gaps: Named fabless, EMS, or end-customer demand
Partner maturity for customer engineering or manufacturing collaboration
